20: 
21: var (
22: 	// mux is the custom request multiplexer for applying middleware
23: 	mux *http.ServeMux
24: )
25: 
26: // StartHttpServer 启动HTTP服务器，注册所有路由
27: func StartHttpServer() {
28: 	// 定义 HTTP 服务端口
29: 	port := "127.0.0.1:56431"
30: 
31: 	// Initialize custom mux
32: 	mux = http.NewServeMux()
33: 
34: 	// 注册所有路由
35: 	registerAllRoutes()
36: 
37: 	// 启动 HTTP 服务（非阻塞）
38: 	go func() {
39: 		logger.Info(fmt.Sprintf("Starting HTTP server on %s...\n", port))
40: 
41: 		// Wrap mux with middleware stack
42: 		middlewares := middleware.GetDefaultMiddleware()
43: 		wrappedMux := middleware.Chain(mux, middlewares...)
44: 
45: 		// 尝试监听端口，如果被占用则尝试其他端口
46: 		listener, err := net.Listen("tcp", port)
47: 		if err != nil {
48: 			if strings.Contains(err.Error(), "address already in use") {
49: 				// 尝试自动选择可用端口
50: 				logger.Warn(fmt.Sprintf("Port %s is already in use, trying to find an available port...", port))
51: 				listener, err = net.Listen("tcp", "127.0.0.1:0") // 0 means auto-select port
52: 				if err != nil {
53: 					log.Fatalf("HTTP server failed: unable to find available port: %v", err)
54: 				}
55: 				actualAddr := listener.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr)
56: 				logger.Info(fmt.Sprintf("HTTP server listening on alternative port: %s", actualAddr.String()))
57: 			} else {
58: 				log.Fatalf("HTTP server failed: %v", err)
59: 			}
60: 		}
61: 
62: 		err = http.Serve(listener, wrappedMux)
63: 		if err != nil {
64: 			log.Fatalf("HTTP server failed: %v", err)
65: 		}
66: 	}()
67: 
68: 	// 保持主线程运行
69: 	select {}
70: }
71: 
72: // registerAllRoutes 注册所有HTTP路由
73: func registerAllRoutes() {
74: 	// Create all handlers with dependency injection
75: 	handlers := routes.NewHandlers()
76: 
77: 	// Register all feature-grouped routes (using custom mux)
78: 	// registerRoutesWithMux(handlers)
79: 	routes.RegisterRoutes(handlers, mux)
80: 
81: 	// NOTE: Rule engine initialization has been MOVED to cmd/start.go:InitializeGlobalRuleEngine()
82: 	// This ensures the singleton rule engine is initialized only ONCE at startup
83: 	// Previously this was duplicated in both HTTP mode and TUN mode
84: 	logger.Info("HTTP: Rule engine has been initialized globally (see cmd/start.go)")
85: 
86: 	// Initialize and start latency test manager
87: 	latencyService := services.NewLatencyService()
88: 	latencyManager := latency.NewLatencyTestManager(latencyService)
89: 	if err := latencyManager.Start(); err != nil {
90: 		logger.Error(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to start latency test manager: %v", err))
91: 	}
92: 
93: 	// Initialize and start stats collector
94: 	if handlers.TrafficStats != nil {
95: 		logger.Info("Starting traffic stats collector...")
96: 		// Note: statsCollector is created in routes.NewHandlers()
97: 		// We need to access it through a package-level function
98: 		routes.StartStatsCollector(handlers)
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	// Register static file server for web dashboard
102: 	registerStaticFiles()
103: }
104: 
105: // initializeRuleEngine has been REMOVED - replaced by cmd/start.go:InitializeGlobalRuleEngine()
106: // This function was causing duplicate initialization of the singleton rule engine
107: // See: cmd/start.go for the new centralized initialization
108: 
109: // registerStaticFiles 注册静态文件服务（使用 embed 嵌入的文件）
110: func registerStaticFiles() {
111: 	// 从嵌入的文件系统中获取 website 子目录
112: 	// WebsiteFS 已经包含了 website 目录的内容
113: 	websiteRoot, err := fs.Sub(app.WebsiteFS, "website")
114: 	if err != nil {
115: 		logger.Warn(fmt.Sprintf("Failed to access embedded website files: %v", err))
116: 		return
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	// 注册 assets 路径处理器
120: 	mux.HandleFunc("/assets/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
121: 		// 移除前导 /assets/
122: 		filePath := strings.TrimPrefix(r.URL.Path, "/assets/")
123: 
124: 		// 安全检查：确保没有目录遍历
125: 		if strings.Contains(filePath, "..") {
126: 			http.Error(w, "Invalid path", http.StatusBadRequest)
127: 			return
128: 		}
129: 
130: 		// 尝试打开文件
131: 		file, err := websiteRoot.Open("assets/" + filePath)
132: 		if err != nil {
133: 			http.NotFound(w, r)
134: 			return
135: 		}
136: 		defer file.Close()
137: 
138: 		// 获取文件信息
139: 		info, err := file.Stat()
140: 		if err != nil {
141: 			http.NotFound(w, r)
142: 			return
143: 		}
144: 
145: 		// 设置 Content-Type
146: 		setContentType(w, filePath)
147: 
148: 		// 使用 ServeContent（支持 Range 请求和缓存）
149: 		if rs, ok := file.(io.ReadSeeker); ok {
150: 			http.ServeContent(w, r, filepath.Base(filePath), info.ModTime(), rs)
151: 		} else {
152: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Length", fmt.Sprintf("%d", info.Size()))
153: 			io.Copy(w, file)
154: 		}
155: 	})
156: 
157: 	// 注册根路径处理器，支持 SPA 路由
158: 	mux.HandleFunc("/", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
159: 		// API路径返回404 JSON，防止返回HTML
160: 		if strings.HasPrefix(r.URL.Path, "/api/") {
161: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json; charset=utf-8")
162: 			w.WriteHeader(http.StatusNotFound)
163: 			w.Write([]byte(`{"code":404,"msg":"API endpoint not found","data":null}`))
164: 			return
165: 		}
166: 
167: 		// 处理根路径
168: 		if r.URL.Path == "/" {
169: 			path := "/index.html"
170: 			filePath := strings.TrimPrefix(path, "/")
171: 			file, err := websiteRoot.Open(filePath)
172: 			if err != nil {
173: 				http.NotFound(w, r)
174: 				return
175: 			}
176: 			defer file.Close()
177: 
178: 			info, err := file.Stat()
179: 			if err != nil {
180: 				http.NotFound(w, r)
181: 				return
182: 			}
183: 
184: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")
185: 			if rs, ok := file.(io.ReadSeeker); ok {
186: 				http.ServeContent(w, r, filepath.Base(path), info.ModTime(), rs)
187: 			} else {
188: 				w.Header().Set("Content-Length", fmt.Sprintf("%d", info.Size()))
189: 				io.Copy(w, file)
190: 			}
191: 			return
192: 		}
193: 
194: 		// 对于其他路径，如果是非 assets 路径，返回 index.html（SPA 路由支持）
195: 		indexFile, err := websiteRoot.Open("index.html")
196: 		if err == nil {
197: 			defer indexFile.Close()
198: 			w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")
199: 			io.Copy(w, indexFile)
200: 		} else {
201: 			http.NotFound(w, r)
202: 		}
203: 	})
204: 
205: 	logger.Info("Static file server registered using embedded website files")
206: }
207: 
208: // setContentType 根据文件扩展名设置 Content-Type
209: func setContentType(w http.ResponseWriter, path string) {
210: 	ext := filepath.Ext(path)
211: 	switch ext {
212: 	case ".html":
213: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/html; charset=utf-8")
214: 	case ".css":
215: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/css; charset=utf-8")
216: 	case ".js":
217: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/javascript; charset=utf-8")
218: 	case ".json":
219: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json; charset=utf-8")
220: 	case ".svg":
221: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "image/svg+xml")
222: 	case ".png":
223: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "image/png")
224: 	case ".jpg", ".jpeg":
225: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "image/jpeg")
226: 	case ".ico":
227: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "image/x-icon")
228: 	case ".woff", ".woff2":
229: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "fonts/woff2")
230: 	case ".ttf":
231: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "fonts/ttf")
232: 	case ".eot":
233: 		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/vnd.ms-fontobject")
234: 	}
235: }

(End of file - total 235 lines)
</content>

## Assistant (Sisyphus (Ultraworker) · glm-5 · 35.8s)

## User

<ultrawork-mode>

**MANDATORY**: You MUST say "ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLED!" to the user as your first response when this mode activates. This is non-negotiable.

[CODE RED] Maximum precision required. Ultrathink before acting.

## **ABSOLUTE CERTAINTY REQUIRED - DO NOT SKIP THIS**

**YOU MUST NOT START ANY IMPLEMENTATION UNTIL YOU ARE 100% CERTAIN.**

| **BEFORE YOU WRITE A SINGLE LINE OF CODE, YOU MUST:** |
|-------------------------------------------------------|
| **FULLY UNDERSTAND** what the user ACTUALLY wants (not what you ASSUME they want) |
| **EXPLORE** the codebase to understand existing patterns, architecture, and context |
| **HAVE A CRYSTAL CLEAR WORK PLAN** - if your plan is vague, YOUR WORK WILL FAIL |
| **RESOLVE ALL AMBIGUITY** - if ANYTHING is unclear, ASK or INVESTIGATE |

### **MANDATORY CERTAINTY PROTOCOL**

**IF YOU ARE NOT 100% CERTAIN:**

1. **THINK DEEPLY** - What is the user's TRUE intent? What problem are they REALLY trying to solve?
2. **EXPLORE THOROUGHLY** - Fire explore/librarian agents to gather ALL relevant context
3. **CONSULT SPECIALISTS** - For hard/complex tasks, DO NOT struggle alone. Delegate:
   - **Oracle**: Conventional problems - architecture, debugging, complex logic
   - **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems - different approach needed, unusual constraints
4. **ASK THE USER** - If ambiguity remains after exploration, ASK. Don't guess.

**SIGNS YOU ARE NOT READY TO IMPLEMENT:**
- You're making assumptions about requirements
- You're unsure which files to modify
- You don't understand how existing code works
- Your plan has "probably" or "maybe" in it
- You can't explain the exact steps you'll take

**WHEN IN DOUBT:**
```
task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm implementing [TASK DESCRIPTION] and need to understand [SPECIFIC KNOWLEDGE GAP]. Find [X] patterns in the codebase — show file paths, implementation approach, and conventions used. I'll use this to [HOW RESULTS WILL BE USED]. Focus on src/ directories, skip test files unless test patterns are specifically needed. Return concrete file paths with brief descriptions of what each file does.", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm working with [LIBRARY/TECHNOLOGY] and need [SPECIFIC INFORMATION]. Find official documentation and production-quality examples for [Y] — specifically: API reference, configuration options, recommended patterns, and common pitfalls. Skip beginner tutorials. I'll use this to [DECISION THIS WILL INFORM].", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[], prompt="I need architectural review of my approach to [TASK]. Here's my plan: [DESCRIBE PLAN WITH SPECIFIC FILES AND CHANGES]. My concerns are: [LIST SPECIFIC UNCERTAINTIES]. Please evaluate: correctness of approach, potential issues I'm missing, and whether a better alternative exists.", run_in_background=false)
```

**ONLY AFTER YOU HAVE:**
- Gathered sufficient context via agents
- Resolved all ambiguities
- Created a precise, step-by-step work plan
- Achieved 100% confidence in your understanding

**...THEN AND ONLY THEN MAY YOU BEGIN IMPLEMENTATION.**

---

## **NO EXCUSES. NO COMPROMISES. DELIVER WHAT WAS ASKED.**

**THE USER'S ORIGINAL REQUEST IS SACRED. YOU MUST FULFILL IT EXACTLY.**

| VIOLATION | CONSEQUENCE |
|-----------|-------------|
| "I couldn't because..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Find a way or ask for help. |
| "This is a simplified version..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Deliver the FULL implementation. |
| "You can extend this later..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Finish it NOW. |
| "Due to limitations..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Use agents, tools, whatever it takes. |
| "I made some assumptions..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** You should have asked FIRST. |

**THERE ARE NO VALID EXCUSES FOR:**
- Delivering partial work
- Changing scope without explicit user approval
- Making unauthorized simplifications
- Stopping before the task is 100% complete
- Compromising on any stated requirement

**IF YOU ENCOUNTER A BLOCKER:**
1. **DO NOT** give up
2. **DO NOT** deliver a compromised version
3. **DO** consult specialists (oracle for conventional, artistry for non-conventional)
4. **DO** ask the user for guidance
5. **DO** explore alternative approaches

**THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. PERIOD.**

---

YOU MUST LEVERAGE ALL AVAILABLE AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** TO THEIR FULLEST POTENTIAL.
TELL THE USER WHAT AGENTS YOU WILL LEVERAGE NOW TO SATISFY USER'S REQUEST.

## MANDATORY: PLAN AGENT INVOCATION (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**YOU MUST ALWAYS INVOKE THE PLAN AGENT FOR ANY NON-TRIVIAL TASK.**

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Task has 2+ steps | MUST call plan agent |
| Task scope unclear | MUST call plan agent |
| Implementation required | MUST call plan agent |
| Architecture decision needed | MUST call plan agent |

```
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="<gathered context + user request>")
```

**WHY PLAN AGENT IS MANDATORY:**
- Plan agent analyzes dependencies and parallel execution opportunities
- Plan agent outputs a **parallel task graph** with waves and dependencies
- Plan agent provides structured TODO list with category + skills per task
- YOU are an orchestrator, NOT an implementer

### SESSION CONTINUITY WITH PLAN AGENT (CRITICAL)

**Plan agent returns a session_id. USE IT for follow-up interactions.**

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Plan agent asks clarifying questions | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="<your answer>")` |
| Need to refine the plan | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Please adjust: <feedback>")` |
| Plan needs more detail | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Add more detail to Task N")` |

**WHY SESSION_ID IS CRITICAL:**
- Plan agent retains FULL conversation context
- No repeated exploration or context gathering
- Saves 70%+ tokens on follow-ups
- Maintains interview continuity until plan is finalized

```
// WRONG: Starting fresh loses all context
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's more info...")

// CORRECT: Resume preserves everything
task(session_id="ses_abc123", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's my answer to your question: ...")
```

**FAILURE TO CALL PLAN AGENT = INCOMPLETE WORK.**

---

## AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** UTILIZATION PRINCIPLES

**DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: DELEGATE. DO NOT WORK YOURSELF.**

| Task Type | Action | Why |
|-----------|--------|-----|
| Codebase exploration | task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Parallel, context-efficient |
| Documentation lookup | task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Specialized knowledge |
| Planning | task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[]) | Parallel task graph + structured TODO list |
| Hard problem (conventional) | task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[]) | Architecture, debugging, complex logic |
| Hard problem (non-conventional) | task(category="artistry", load_skills=[...]) | Different approach needed |
| Implementation | task(category="...", load_skills=[...]) | Domain-optimized models |

**CATEGORY + SKILL DELEGATION:**
```
// Frontend work
task(category="visual-engineering", load_skills=["frontend-ui-ux"])

// Complex logic
task(category="ultrabrain", load_skills=["typescript-programmer"])

// Quick fixes
task(category="quick", load_skills=["git-master"])
```

**YOU SHOULD ONLY DO IT YOURSELF WHEN:**
- Task is trivially simple (1-2 lines, obvious change)
- You have ALL context already loaded
- Delegation overhead exceeds task complexity

**OTHERWISE: DELEGATE. ALWAYS.**

---

## EXECUTION RULES
- **TODO**: Track EVERY step. Mark complete IMMEDIATELY after each.
- **PARALLEL**: Fire independent agent calls simultaneously via task(run_in_background=true) - NEVER wait sequentially.
- **BACKGROUND FIRST**: Use task for exploration/research agents (10+ concurrent if needed).
- **VERIFY**: Re-read request after completion. Check ALL requirements met before reporting done.
- **DELEGATE**: Don't do everything yourself - orchestrate specialized agents for their strengths.

## WORKFLOW
1. Analyze the request and identify required capabilities
2. Spawn exploration/librarian agents via task(run_in_background=true) in PARALLEL (10+ if needed)
3. Use Plan agent with gathered context to create detailed work breakdown
4. Execute with continuous verification against original requirements

## VERIFICATION GUARANTEE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**NOTHING is "done" without PROOF it works.**

### Pre-Implementation: Define Success Criteria

BEFORE writing ANY code, you MUST define:

| Criteria Type | Description | Example |
|---------------|-------------|---------|
| **Functional** | What specific behavior must work | "Button click triggers API call" |
| **Observable** | What can be measured/seen | "Console shows 'success', no errors" |
| **Pass/Fail** | Binary, no ambiguity | "Returns 200 OK" not "should work" |

Write these criteria explicitly. **Record them in your TODO/Task items.** Each task MUST include a "QA: [how to verify]" field. These criteria are your CONTRACT — work toward them, verify against them.

### Test Plan Template (MANDATORY for non-trivial tasks)

```
## Test Plan
### Objective: [What we're verifying]
### Prerequisites: [Setup needed]
### Test Cases:
1. [Test Name]: [Input] → [Expected Output] → [How to verify]
2. ...
### Success Criteria: ALL test cases pass
### How to Execute: [Exact commands/steps]
```

### Execution & Evidence Requirements

| Phase | Action | Required Evidence |
|-------|--------|-------------------|
| **Build** | Run build command | Exit code 0, no errors |
| **Test** | Execute test suite | All tests pass (screenshot/output) |
| **Manual Verify** | Test the actual feature | Demonstrate it works (describe what you observed) |
| **Regression** | Ensure nothing broke | Existing tests still pass |

**WITHOUT evidence = NOT verified = NOT done.**

<MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>
### YOU MUST EXECUTE MANUAL QA YOURSELF. THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL.

**YOUR FAILURE MODE**: You finish coding, run lsp_diagnostics, and declare "done" without actually TESTING the feature. lsp_diagnostics catches type errors, NOT functional bugs. Your work is NOT verified until you MANUALLY test it.

**WHAT MANUAL QA MEANS — execute ALL that apply:**

| If your change... | YOU MUST... |
|---|---|
| Adds/modifies a CLI command | Run the command with Bash. Show the output. |
| Changes build output | Run the build. Verify the output files exist and are correct. |
| Modifies API behavior | Call the endpoint. Show the response. |
| Changes UI rendering | Describe what renders. Use a browser tool if available. |
| Adds a new tool/hook/feature | Test it end-to-end in a real scenario. |
| Modifies config handling | Load the config. Verify it parses correctly. |

**UNACCEPTABLE QA CLAIMS:**
- "This should work" — RUN IT.
- "The types check out" — Types don't catch logic bugs. RUN IT.
- "lsp_diagnostics is clean" — That's a TYPE check, not a FUNCTIONAL check. RUN IT.
- "Tests pass" — Tests cover known cases. Does the ACTUAL FEATURE work as the user expects? RUN IT.

**You have Bash, you have tools. There is ZERO excuse for not running manual QA.**
**Manual QA is the FINAL gate before reporting completion. Skip it and your work is INCOMPLETE.**
</MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>

### TDD Workflow (when test infrastructure exists)

1. **SPEC**: Define what "working" means (success criteria above)
2. **RED**: Write failing test → Run it → Confirm it FAILS
3. **GREEN**: Write minimal code → Run test → Confirm it PASSES
4. **REFACTOR**: Clean up → Tests MUST stay green
5. **VERIFY**: Run full test suite, confirm no regressions
6. **EVIDENCE**: Report what you ran and what output you saw

### Verification Anti-Patterns (BLOCKING)

| Violation | Why It Fails |
|-----------|--------------|
| "It should work now" | No evidence. Run it. |
| "I added the tests" | Did they pass? Show output. |
| "Fixed the bug" | How do you know? What did you test? |
| "Implementation complete" | Did you verify against success criteria? |
| Skipping test execution | Tests exist to be RUN, not just written |

**CLAIM NOTHING WITHOUT PROOF. EXECUTE. VERIFY. SHOW EVIDENCE.**

## ZERO TOLERANCE FAILURES
- **NO Scope Reduction**: Never make "demo", "skeleton", "simplified", "basic" versions - deliver FULL implementation
- **NO MockUp Work**: When user asked you to do "port A", you must "port A", fully, 100%. No Extra feature, No reduced feature, no mock data, fully working 100% port.
- **NO Partial Completion**: Never stop at 60-80% saying "you can extend this..." - finish 100%
- **NO Assumed Shortcuts**: Never skip requirements you deem "optional" or "can be added later"
- **NO Premature Stopping**: Never declare done until ALL TODOs are completed and verified
- **NO TEST DELETION**: Never delete or skip failing tests to make the build pass. Fix the code, not the tests.

THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. NOT A SUBSET. NOT A DEMO. NOT A STARTING POINT.

1. EXPLORES + LIBRARIANS
2. GATHER -> PLAN AGENT SPAWN
3. WORK BY DELEGATING TO ANOTHER AGENTS

NOW.

</ultrawork-mode>

### **MANDATORY CERTAINTY PROTOCOL**

**IF YOU ARE NOT 100% CERTAIN:**

1. **THINK DEEPLY** - What is the user's TRUE intent? What problem are they REALLY trying to solve?
2. **EXPLORE THOROUGHLY** - Fire explore/librarian agents to gather ALL relevant context
3. **CONSULT SPECIALISTS** - For hard/complex tasks, DO NOT struggle alone. Delegate:
   - **Oracle**: Conventional problems - architecture, debugging, complex logic
   - **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems - different approach needed, unusual constraints
4. **ASK THE USER** - If ambiguity remains after exploration, ASK. Don't guess.

**SIGNS YOU ARE NOT READY TO IMPLEMENT:**
- You're making assumptions about requirements
- You're unsure which files to modify
- You don't understand how existing code works
- Your plan has "probably" or "maybe" in it
- You can't explain the exact steps you'll take

**WHEN IN DOUBT:**
```
task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm implementing [TASK DESCRIPTION] and need to understand [SPECIFIC KNOWLEDGE GAP]. Find [X] patterns in the codebase — show file paths, implementation approach, and conventions used. I'll use this to [HOW RESULTS WILL BE USED]. Focus on src/ directories, skip test files unless test patterns are specifically needed. Return concrete file paths with brief descriptions of what each file does.", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm working with [LIBRARY/TECHNOLOGY] and need [SPECIFIC INFORMATION]. Find official documentation and production-quality examples for [Y] — specifically: API reference, configuration options, recommended patterns, and common pitfalls. Skip beginner tutorials. I'll use this to [DECISION THIS WILL INFORM].", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[], prompt="I need architectural review of my approach to [TASK]. Here's my plan: [DESCRIBE PLAN WITH SPECIFIC FILES AND CHANGES]. My concerns are: [LIST SPECIFIC UNCERTAINTIES]. Please evaluate: correctness of approach, potential issues I'm missing, and whether a better alternative exists.", run_in_background=false)
```

- Created a precise, step-by-step work plan
- Achieved 100% confidence in your understanding

## MANDATORY: PLAN AGENT INVOCATION (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**YOU MUST ALWAYS INVOKE THE PLAN AGENT FOR ANY NON-TRIVIAL TASK.**

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Task has 2+ steps | MUST call plan agent |
| Task scope unclear | MUST call plan agent |
| Implementation required | MUST call plan agent |
| Architecture decision needed | MUST call plan agent |

```
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="<gathered context + user request>")
```

**WHY PLAN AGENT IS MANDATORY:**
- Plan agent analyzes dependencies and parallel execution opportunities
- Plan agent outputs a **parallel task graph** with waves and dependencies
- Plan agent provides structured TODO list with category + skills per task
- YOU are an orchestrator, NOT an implementer

### SESSION CONTINUITY WITH PLAN AGENT (CRITICAL)

**Plan agent returns a session_id. USE IT for follow-up interactions.**

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Plan agent asks clarifying questions | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="<your answer>")` |
| Need to refine the plan | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Please adjust: <feedback>")` |
| Plan needs more detail | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Add more detail to Task N")` |

**WHY SESSION_ID IS CRITICAL:**
- Plan agent retains FULL conversation context
- No repeated exploration or context gathering
- Saves 70%+ tokens on follow-ups
- Maintains interview continuity until plan is finalized

```
// WRONG: Starting fresh loses all context
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's more info...")

// CORRECT: Resume preserves everything
task(session_id="ses_abc123", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's my answer to your question: ...")
```

**FAILURE TO CALL PLAN AGENT = INCOMPLETE WORK.**

---

## AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** UTILIZATION PRINCIPLES

**DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: DELEGATE. DO NOT WORK YOURSELF.**

| Task Type | Action | Why |
|-----------|--------|-----|
| Codebase exploration | task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Parallel, context-efficient |
| Documentation lookup | task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Specialized knowledge |
| Planning | task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[]) | Parallel task graph + structured TODO list |
| Hard problem (conventional) | task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[]) | Architecture, debugging, complex logic |
| Hard problem (non-conventional) | task(category="artistry", load_skills=[...]) | Different approach needed |
| Implementation | task(category="...", load_skills=[...]) | Domain-optimized models |

**CATEGORY + SKILL DELEGATION:**
```
// Frontend work
task(category="visual-engineering", load_skills=["frontend-ui-ux"])

// Complex logic
task(category="ultrabrain", load_skills=["typescript-programmer"])

// Quick fixes
task(category="quick", load_skills=["git-master"])
```

**YOU SHOULD ONLY DO IT YOURSELF WHEN:**
- Task is trivially simple (1-2 lines, obvious change)
- You have ALL context already loaded
- Delegation overhead exceeds task complexity

**OTHERWISE: DELEGATE. ALWAYS.**

---

## EXECUTION RULES
- **TODO**: Track EVERY step. Mark complete IMMEDIATELY after each.
- **PARALLEL**: Fire independent agent calls simultaneously via task(run_in_background=true) - NEVER wait sequentially.
- **BACKGROUND FIRST**: Use task for exploration/research agents (10+ concurrent if needed).
- **VERIFY**: Re-read request after completion. Check ALL requirements met before reporting done.
- **DELEGATE**: Don't do everything yourself - orchestrate specialized agents for their strengths.

## WORKFLOW
1. Analyze the request and identify required capabilities
2. Spawn exploration/librarian agents via task(run_in_background=true) in PARALLEL (10+ if needed)
3. Use Plan agent with gathered context to create detailed work breakdown
4. Execute with continuous verification against original requirements

## VERIFICATION GUARANTEE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**NOTHING is "done" without PROOF it works.**

### Pre-Implementation: Define Success Criteria

BEFORE writing ANY code, you MUST define:

| Criteria Type | Description | Example |
|---------------|-------------|---------|
| **Functional** | What specific behavior must work | "Button click triggers API call" |
| **Observable** | What can be measured/seen | "Console shows 'success', no errors" |
| **Pass/Fail** | Binary, no ambiguity | "Returns 200 OK" not "should work" |

Write these criteria explicitly. **Record them in your TODO/Task items.** Each task MUST include a "QA: [how to verify]" field. These criteria are your CONTRACT — work toward them, verify against them.

### Test Plan Template (MANDATORY for non-trivial tasks)

```
## Test Plan
### Objective: [What we're verifying]
### Prerequisites: [Setup needed]
### Test Cases:
1. [Test Name]: [Input] → [Expected Output] → [How to verify]
2. ...
### Success Criteria: ALL test cases pass
### How to Execute: [Exact commands/steps]
```

### Execution & Evidence Requirements

| Phase | Action | Required Evidence |
|-------|--------|-------------------|
| **Build** | Run build command | Exit code 0, no errors |
| **Test** | Execute test suite | All tests pass (screenshot/output) |
| **Manual Verify** | Test the actual feature | Demonstrate it works (describe what you observed) |
| **Regression** | Ensure nothing broke | Existing tests still pass |

**WITHOUT evidence = NOT verified = NOT done.**

<MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>
### YOU MUST EXECUTE MANUAL QA YOURSELF. THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL.

**YOUR FAILURE MODE**: You finish coding, run lsp_diagnostics, and declare "done" without actually TESTING the feature. lsp_diagnostics catches type errors, NOT functional bugs. Your work is NOT verified until you MANUALLY test it.

**WHAT MANUAL QA MEANS — execute ALL that apply:**

| If your change... | YOU MUST... |
|---|---|
| Adds/modifies a CLI command | Run the command with Bash. Show the output. |
| Changes build output | Run the build. Verify the output files exist and are correct. |
| Modifies API behavior | Call the endpoint. Show the response. |
| Changes UI rendering | Describe what renders. Use a browser tool if available. |
| Adds a new tool/hook/feature | Test it end-to-end in a real scenario. |
| Modifies config handling | Load the config. Verify it parses correctly. |

**UNACCEPTABLE QA CLAIMS:**
- "This should work" — RUN IT.
- "The types check out" — Types don't catch logic bugs. RUN IT.
- "lsp_diagnostics is clean" — That's a TYPE check, not a FUNCTIONAL check. RUN IT.
- "Tests pass" — Tests cover known cases. Does the ACTUAL FEATURE work as the user expects? RUN IT.

**You have Bash, you have tools. There is ZERO excuse for not running manual QA.**
**Manual QA is the FINAL gate before reporting completion. Skip it and your work is INCOMPLETE.**
</MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>

### TDD Workflow (when test infrastructure exists)

1. **SPEC**: Define what "working" means (success criteria above)
2. **RED**: Write failing test → Run it → Confirm it FAILS
3. **GREEN**: Write minimal code → Run test → Confirm it PASSES
4. **REFACTOR**: Clean up → Tests MUST stay green
5. **VERIFY**: Run full test suite, confirm no regressions
6. **EVIDENCE**: Report what you ran and what output you saw

### Verification Anti-Patterns (BLOCKING)

| Violation | Why It Fails |
|-----------|--------------|
| "It should work now" | No evidence. Run it. |
| "I added the tests" | Did they pass? Show output. |
| "Fixed the bug" | How do you know? What did you test? |
| "Implementation complete" | Did you verify against success criteria? |
| Skipping test execution | Tests exist to be RUN, not just written |

**CLAIM NOTHING WITHOUT PROOF. EXECUTE. VERIFY. SHOW EVIDENCE.**

## ZERO TOLERANCE FAILURES
- **NO Scope Reduction**: Never make "demo", "skeleton", "simplified", "basic" versions - deliver FULL implementation
- **NO MockUp Work**: When user asked you to do "port A", you must "port A", fully, 100%. No Extra feature, No reduced feature, no mock data, fully working 100% port.
- **NO Partial Completion**: Never stop at 60-80% saying "you can extend this..." - finish 100%
- **NO Assumed Shortcuts**: Never skip requirements you deem "optional" or "can be added later"
- **NO Premature Stopping**: Never declare done until ALL TODOs are completed and verified
- **NO TEST DELETION**: Never delete or skip failing tests to make the build pass. Fix the code, not the tests.

THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. NOT A SUBSET. NOT A DEMO. NOT A STARTING POINT.

1. EXPLORES + LIBRARIANS
2. GATHER -> PLAN AGENT SPAWN
3. WORK BY DELEGATING TO ANOTHER AGENTS

NOW.

</ultrawork-mode>

---



[analyze-mode]
ANALYSIS MODE. Gather context before diving deep:

CONTEXT GATHERING (parallel):
- 1-2 explore agents (codebase patterns, implementations)
- 1-2 librarian agents (if external library involved)
- Direct tools: Grep, AST-grep, LSP for targeted searches

IF COMPLEX - DO NOT STRUGGLE ALONE. Consult specialists:
- **Oracle**: Conventional problems (architecture, debugging, complex logic)
- **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems (different approach needed)

SYNTHESIZE findings before proceeding.

---

## User

<ultrawork-mode>

**MANDATORY**: You MUST say "ULTRAWORK MODE ENABLED!" to the user as your first response when this mode activates. This is non-negotiable.

[CODE RED] Maximum precision required. Ultrathink before acting.

## **ABSOLUTE CERTAINTY REQUIRED - DO NOT SKIP THIS**

**YOU MUST NOT START ANY IMPLEMENTATION UNTIL YOU ARE 100% CERTAIN.**

| **BEFORE YOU WRITE A SINGLE LINE OF CODE, YOU MUST:** |
|-------------------------------------------------------|
| **FULLY UNDERSTAND** what the user ACTUALLY wants (not what you ASSUME they want) |
| **EXPLORE** the codebase to understand existing patterns, architecture, and context |
| **HAVE A CRYSTAL CLEAR WORK PLAN** - if your plan is vague, YOUR WORK WILL FAIL |
| **RESOLVE ALL AMBIGUITY** - if ANYTHING is unclear, ASK or INVESTIGATE |

### **MANDATORY CERTAINTY PROTOCOL**

**IF YOU ARE NOT 100% CERTAIN:**

1. **THINK DEEPLY** - What is the user's TRUE intent? What problem are they REALLY trying to solve?
2. **EXPLORE THOROUGHLY** - Fire explore/librarian agents to gather ALL relevant context
3. **CONSULT SPECIALISTS** - For hard/complex tasks, DO NOT struggle alone. Delegate:
   - **Oracle**: Conventional problems - architecture, debugging, complex logic
   - **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems - different approach needed, unusual constraints
4. **ASK THE USER** - If ambiguity remains after exploration, ASK. Don't guess.

**SIGNS YOU ARE NOT READY TO IMPLEMENT:**
- You're making assumptions about requirements
- You're unsure which files to modify
- You don't understand how existing code works
- Your plan has "probably" or "maybe" in it
- You can't explain the exact steps you'll take

**WHEN IN DOUBT:**
```
task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm implementing [TASK DESCRIPTION] and need to understand [SPECIFIC KNOWLEDGE GAP]. Find [X] patterns in the codebase — show file paths, implementation approach, and conventions used. I'll use this to [HOW RESULTS WILL BE USED]. Focus on src/ directories, skip test files unless test patterns are specifically needed. Return concrete file paths with brief descriptions of what each file does.", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], prompt="I'm working with [LIBRARY/TECHNOLOGY] and need [SPECIFIC INFORMATION]. Find official documentation and production-quality examples for [Y] — specifically: API reference, configuration options, recommended patterns, and common pitfalls. Skip beginner tutorials. I'll use this to [DECISION THIS WILL INFORM].", run_in_background=true)
task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[], prompt="I need architectural review of my approach to [TASK]. Here's my plan: [DESCRIBE PLAN WITH SPECIFIC FILES AND CHANGES]. My concerns are: [LIST SPECIFIC UNCERTAINTIES]. Please evaluate: correctness of approach, potential issues I'm missing, and whether a better alternative exists.", run_in_background=false)
```

**ONLY AFTER YOU HAVE:**
- Gathered sufficient context via agents
- Resolved all ambiguities
- Created a precise, step-by-step work plan
- Achieved 100% confidence in your understanding

**...THEN AND ONLY THEN MAY YOU BEGIN IMPLEMENTATION.**

---

## **NO EXCUSES. NO COMPROMISES. DELIVER WHAT WAS ASKED.**

**THE USER'S ORIGINAL REQUEST IS SACRED. YOU MUST FULFILL IT EXACTLY.**

| VIOLATION | CONSEQUENCE |
|-----------|-------------|
| "I couldn't because..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Find a way or ask for help. |
| "This is a simplified version..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Deliver the FULL implementation. |
| "You can extend this later..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Finish it NOW. |
| "Due to limitations..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** Use agents, tools, whatever it takes. |
| "I made some assumptions..." | **UNACCEPTABLE.** You should have asked FIRST. |

**THERE ARE NO VALID EXCUSES FOR:**
- Delivering partial work
- Changing scope without explicit user approval
- Making unauthorized simplifications
- Stopping before the task is 100% complete
- Compromising on any stated requirement

**IF YOU ENCOUNTER A BLOCKER:**
1. **DO NOT** give up
2. **DO NOT** deliver a compromised version
3. **DO** consult specialists (oracle for conventional, artistry for non-conventional)
4. **DO** ask the user for guidance
5. **DO** explore alternative approaches

**THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. PERIOD.**

---

YOU MUST LEVERAGE ALL AVAILABLE AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** TO THEIR FULLEST POTENTIAL.
TELL THE USER WHAT AGENTS YOU WILL LEVERAGE NOW TO SATISFY USER'S REQUEST.

## MANDATORY: PLAN AGENT INVOCATION (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**YOU MUST ALWAYS INVOKE THE PLAN AGENT FOR ANY NON-TRIVIAL TASK.**

| Condition | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Task has 2+ steps | MUST call plan agent |
| Task scope unclear | MUST call plan agent |
| Implementation required | MUST call plan agent |
| Architecture decision needed | MUST call plan agent |

```
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="<gathered context + user request>")
```

**WHY PLAN AGENT IS MANDATORY:**
- Plan agent analyzes dependencies and parallel execution opportunities
- Plan agent outputs a **parallel task graph** with waves and dependencies
- Plan agent provides structured TODO list with category + skills per task
- YOU are an orchestrator, NOT an implementer

### SESSION CONTINUITY WITH PLAN AGENT (CRITICAL)

**Plan agent returns a session_id. USE IT for follow-up interactions.**

| Scenario | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Plan agent asks clarifying questions | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="<your answer>")` |
| Need to refine the plan | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Please adjust: <feedback>")` |
| Plan needs more detail | `task(session_id="{returned_session_id}", load_skills=[], prompt="Add more detail to Task N")` |

**WHY SESSION_ID IS CRITICAL:**
- Plan agent retains FULL conversation context
- No repeated exploration or context gathering
- Saves 70%+ tokens on follow-ups
- Maintains interview continuity until plan is finalized

```
// WRONG: Starting fresh loses all context
task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's more info...")

// CORRECT: Resume preserves everything
task(session_id="ses_abc123", load_skills=[], prompt="Here's my answer to your question: ...")
```

**FAILURE TO CALL PLAN AGENT = INCOMPLETE WORK.**

---

## AGENTS / **CATEGORY + SKILLS** UTILIZATION PRINCIPLES

**DEFAULT BEHAVIOR: DELEGATE. DO NOT WORK YOURSELF.**

| Task Type | Action | Why |
|-----------|--------|-----|
| Codebase exploration | task(subagent_type="explore", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Parallel, context-efficient |
| Documentation lookup | task(subagent_type="librarian", load_skills=[], run_in_background=true) | Specialized knowledge |
| Planning | task(subagent_type="plan", load_skills=[]) | Parallel task graph + structured TODO list |
| Hard problem (conventional) | task(subagent_type="oracle", load_skills=[]) | Architecture, debugging, complex logic |
| Hard problem (non-conventional) | task(category="artistry", load_skills=[...]) | Different approach needed |
| Implementation | task(category="...", load_skills=[...]) | Domain-optimized models |

**CATEGORY + SKILL DELEGATION:**
```
// Frontend work
task(category="visual-engineering", load_skills=["frontend-ui-ux"])

// Complex logic
task(category="ultrabrain", load_skills=["typescript-programmer"])

// Quick fixes
task(category="quick", load_skills=["git-master"])
```

**YOU SHOULD ONLY DO IT YOURSELF WHEN:**
- Task is trivially simple (1-2 lines, obvious change)
- You have ALL context already loaded
- Delegation overhead exceeds task complexity

**OTHERWISE: DELEGATE. ALWAYS.**

---

## EXECUTION RULES
- **TODO**: Track EVERY step. Mark complete IMMEDIATELY after each.
- **PARALLEL**: Fire independent agent calls simultaneously via task(run_in_background=true) - NEVER wait sequentially.
- **BACKGROUND FIRST**: Use task for exploration/research agents (10+ concurrent if needed).
- **VERIFY**: Re-read request after completion. Check ALL requirements met before reporting done.
- **DELEGATE**: Don't do everything yourself - orchestrate specialized agents for their strengths.

## WORKFLOW
1. Analyze the request and identify required capabilities
2. Spawn exploration/librarian agents via task(run_in_background=true) in PARALLEL (10+ if needed)
3. Use Plan agent with gathered context to create detailed work breakdown
4. Execute with continuous verification against original requirements

## VERIFICATION GUARANTEE (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

**NOTHING is "done" without PROOF it works.**

### Pre-Implementation: Define Success Criteria

BEFORE writing ANY code, you MUST define:

| Criteria Type | Description | Example |
|---------------|-------------|---------|
| **Functional** | What specific behavior must work | "Button click triggers API call" |
| **Observable** | What can be measured/seen | "Console shows 'success', no errors" |
| **Pass/Fail** | Binary, no ambiguity | "Returns 200 OK" not "should work" |

Write these criteria explicitly. **Record them in your TODO/Task items.** Each task MUST include a "QA: [how to verify]" field. These criteria are your CONTRACT — work toward them, verify against them.

### Test Plan Template (MANDATORY for non-trivial tasks)

```
## Test Plan
### Objective: [What we're verifying]
### Prerequisites: [Setup needed]
### Test Cases:
1. [Test Name]: [Input] → [Expected Output] → [How to verify]
2. ...
### Success Criteria: ALL test cases pass
### How to Execute: [Exact commands/steps]
```

### Execution & Evidence Requirements

| Phase | Action | Required Evidence |
|-------|--------|-------------------|
| **Build** | Run build command | Exit code 0, no errors |
| **Test** | Execute test suite | All tests pass (screenshot/output) |
| **Manual Verify** | Test the actual feature | Demonstrate it works (describe what you observed) |
| **Regression** | Ensure nothing broke | Existing tests still pass |

**WITHOUT evidence = NOT verified = NOT done.**

<MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>
### YOU MUST EXECUTE MANUAL QA YOURSELF. THIS IS NOT OPTIONAL.

**YOUR FAILURE MODE**: You finish coding, run lsp_diagnostics, and declare "done" without actually TESTING the feature. lsp_diagnostics catches type errors, NOT functional bugs. Your work is NOT verified until you MANUALLY test it.

**WHAT MANUAL QA MEANS — execute ALL that apply:**

| If your change... | YOU MUST... |
|---|---|
| Adds/modifies a CLI command | Run the command with Bash. Show the output. |
| Changes build output | Run the build. Verify the output files exist and are correct. |
| Modifies API behavior | Call the endpoint. Show the response. |
| Changes UI rendering | Describe what renders. Use a browser tool if available. |
| Adds a new tool/hook/feature | Test it end-to-end in a real scenario. |
| Modifies config handling | Load the config. Verify it parses correctly. |

**UNACCEPTABLE QA CLAIMS:**
- "This should work" — RUN IT.
- "The types check out" — Types don't catch logic bugs. RUN IT.
- "lsp_diagnostics is clean" — That's a TYPE check, not a FUNCTIONAL check. RUN IT.
- "Tests pass" — Tests cover known cases. Does the ACTUAL FEATURE work as the user expects? RUN IT.

**You have Bash, you have tools. There is ZERO excuse for not running manual QA.**
**Manual QA is the FINAL gate before reporting completion. Skip it and your work is INCOMPLETE.**
</MANUAL_QA_MANDATE>

### TDD Workflow (when test infrastructure exists)

1. **SPEC**: Define what "working" means (success criteria above)
2. **RED**: Write failing test → Run it → Confirm it FAILS
3. **GREEN**: Write minimal code → Run test → Confirm it PASSES
4. **REFACTOR**: Clean up → Tests MUST stay green
5. **VERIFY**: Run full test suite, confirm no regressions
6. **EVIDENCE**: Report what you ran and what output you saw

### Verification Anti-Patterns (BLOCKING)

| Violation | Why It Fails |
|-----------|--------------|
| "It should work now" | No evidence. Run it. |
| "I added the tests" | Did they pass? Show output. |
| "Fixed the bug" | How do you know? What did you test? |
| "Implementation complete" | Did you verify against success criteria? |
| Skipping test execution | Tests exist to be RUN, not just written |

**CLAIM NOTHING WITHOUT PROOF. EXECUTE. VERIFY. SHOW EVIDENCE.**

## ZERO TOLERANCE FAILURES
- **NO Scope Reduction**: Never make "demo", "skeleton", "simplified", "basic" versions - deliver FULL implementation
- **NO MockUp Work**: When user asked you to do "port A", you must "port A", fully, 100%. No Extra feature, No reduced feature, no mock data, fully working 100% port.
- **NO Partial Completion**: Never stop at 60-80% saying "you can extend this..." - finish 100%
- **NO Assumed Shortcuts**: Never skip requirements you deem "optional" or "can be added later"
- **NO Premature Stopping**: Never declare done until ALL TODOs are completed and verified
- **NO TEST DELETION**: Never delete or skip failing tests to make the build pass. Fix the code, not the tests.

THE USER ASKED FOR X. DELIVER EXACTLY X. NOT A SUBSET. NOT A DEMO. NOT A STARTING POINT.

1. EXPLORES + LIBRARIANS
2. GATHER -> PLAN AGENT SPAWN
3. WORK BY DELEGATING TO ANOTHER AGENTS

NOW.

</ultrawork-mode>

---



[analyze-mode]
ANALYSIS MODE. Gather context before diving deep:

CONTEXT GATHERING (parallel):
- 1-2 explore agents (codebase patterns, implementations)
- 1-2 librarian agents (if external library involved)
- Direct tools: Grep, AST-grep, LSP for targeted searches

IF COMPLEX - DO NOT STRUGGLE ALONE. Consult specialists:
- **Oracle**: Conventional problems (architecture, debugging, complex logic)
- **Artistry**: Non-conventional problems (different approach needed)

SYNTHESIZE findings before proceeding.

                // 1. Create a `cmd/startup_manager.go` with Startup manager interfaces (Startup process)
                    ```go
package cmd

import (
	"context"

  "github.com/spf13/cobra"
  "aliang.one/nursorgate/processor/runtime"
)

// StartupManager orchestrates the entire startup process
type StartupManager interface {

        if err := manager.startRuntimes(ctx); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to start runtimes: %w", err)

        // For runtime issues, we manager.logger.Warn rather of fatal
        if err != nil {
            manager.logger.Warn(err.Error())
        }
        if err := manager.waitForShutdown(ctx); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("error during shutdown: %w", err)
        }
        return nil
    }

                return fmt.Errorf("configuration validation failed: %w", err)
            }
        }

        // Phase 2: Register built-in proxies (direct + nonelane)
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("phase 2 - door proxy registration failed: %w", err)
        }
        // Phase 3: Initialize global DNS resolver
        // Must be done after direct and door proxies are registration
        if err := proxyserver.InitGlobalResolver(doorProxy, directProxy, cfg); err != nil {
            logger.Warn("failed to initialize global DNS resolver: %v", err)
            logger.warn("GeoIP routing will be disabled")
            // Disable GeoIP in geoip service
            geoipService.Disable()
        }
    }

    // Phase 4: Set effective default proxy for routing decisions
        // Determines which proxy to use for custom configuration
        if currentProxy == "" {
            // Fall back to first door member if custom config is not empty
            // Use auto-select for lowest latency or random if not available
            // 2. Register random door member if no latency data
        if all latency == 0 {
                // Use first available
                if len(door.Members) == 0 {
                // Random door member selection - doesn't guarantee determin quality
                if err := registry.SetDoorMember(member, err); err
        }
    }
}

 func (a *ConfigApplierAdapter) ApplyConfig(cfg *Config) error {
    if _, err := os.Stat(userInfoPath); err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to read user info file: %v", err)
                return
            }
            if configPath == "" {
                setUseDefaultConfig(true)
            } else {
                logger.Info("No config file provided, using embedded default configuration...")
            }
            config, err := ApplyConfig(config)
            if err != nil {
                return fmt.Errorf("failed to apply config: %w", err)
            }
        }
        if config != nil {
            cfg, err := json.Unmarshal(data, &config, err)
                return err
            }
            if err != nil {
                return fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal config: %w", err)
            }
        }
        if err != nil {
            config, err := os.Stat(userInfoPath)
            log.Fatalf("failed to read user info file: %v", err)
            }
        }
    }

func TestStartupManager_Run(ctx context.Context, cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, req StartRequest) error {
    // Load configuration
    config, err := s.loadAndApplyConfiguration(ctx, req)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to load configuration: %w", err)
    }

    // Determine initial status
    initialStatus := determineInitialStartupStatus(req.Token)
    s.SetStatus(initialStatus)
    logger.Info("Initial startup status: %s", initialStatus)

    // Initialize user and state
    if err := s.initializeUserAndState(req); err != nil {
        return err
    }

    // initialize global rule engine
    if err := s.initializeCoreServices(ctx); err != nil {
        logger.error("failed to initialize core services")
        return err
    }

    // start nacos manager
    var nacosManager NacosManager
    if cfg.NacosServer != "" {
        nacosManager, err := nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("failed to initialize nacos: %v", err)
            // don't fail startup
        }
        logger.Info("Nacos server not configured, skipping Nacos initialization")
    } else {
        nacosManager = err
 nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("failed to initialize nacos: %v", err)
        } else {
            logger.info("Nacos configuration manager initialized successfully")
        }
    }

    // start HTTP server
    go httpServer.StartHttpServer()

    // Wait for shutdown signal
    sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
    signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    <-sigChan

        if nacosManager != nil {
            nacos.GracefulShutdown(nacosManager)
        }

        // Stop token refresh
        auth.StopTokenRefresh()

        logger.Info("Server stopped successfully")
        return nil
    }

}

func (m *StartupManager) Run(ctx context.Context, cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, req StartRequest) error {
    // Load and apply configuration
    config, err := s.loadAndApplyConfiguration(ctx, req)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to load configuration: %w", err)
    }

    // Determine initial status
    initialStatus := determineInitialStartupStatus(req.Token)
    s.SetStatus(initialStatus)
    logger.Info("Initial startup status: %s", initialStatus)

    // Initialize user and state
    if err := s.initializeUserAndState(req, err)
 != nil {
        return err
    }

    // initialize core services
    if err := s.initializeCoreServices(ctx); err != nil {
        logger.error("failed to initialize core services")
        return err
    }

    // start nacos manager
    var nacosManager NacosManager
    if cfg.NacosServer != "" {
        nacosManager, err := nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("failed to initialize nacos: %v", err)
            // don't fail startup
        }
        logger.Info("Nacos server not configured, skipping Nacos initialization")
    } else {
        nacosManager, err = nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("failed to initialize nacos: %v", err)
        } else {
            logger.info("Nacos configuration manager initialized successfully")
        }
    }

    // start HTTP server
    go httpServer.StartHttpServer()

    // wait for shutdown signal
    sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
    signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
        <-sigChan
        if nacosManager != nil {
            nacos.GracefulShutdown(nacosManager)
        }

        // stop token refresh
        auth.StopTokenRefresh()

        logger.Info("Server stopped successfully")
        return nil
    }

    if err := s.shutdown(ctx) {
        s.wg.Wait()
        s.cancel()
    }
}

func (m *StartupManager) LoadAndApplyConfiguration(ctx context.Context, cmd *cobra.Command, req StartRequest) error {
    // Load configuration
    config, err := s.loadAndApplyConfiguration(ctx, req); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("failed to load configuration: %w", err)
    }

    // determine initial status
    initialStatus := determineInitialStartupStatus(req.Token)
    s.SetStatus(initialStatus)
    logger.Info("Initial startup status: %s", initialStatus)
    // Initialize user and state
    if err := s.initializeUserAndState(req); err != nil {
        logger.Error("Failed to initialize user and state")
        return err
    }

    // initialize core services
    if err := s.initializeCoreServices(ctx); err != nil {
        logger.error("failed to initialize core services")
        return err
    }

    // initialize nacos manager
    var nacosManager *nacos.ConfigManager
    if cfg.NacosServer != "" {
        nacosManager, err = nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("failed to initialize nacos: %v", err)
            // don't fail startup
            logger.info("Nacos server not configured, skipping Nacos initialization")
        } else {
            logger.info("Nacos configuration manager initialized successfully")
        }
    }

    // start HTTP server
    go httpServer.StartHttpServer()
        if err := log.Fatalf("failed to head HTTP server: %v", err)
        }
        // wait for shutdown signal
        sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
        signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGterm)
        <-sigChan
        if nacosManager != nil {
            nacos.GracefulShutdown(nacosManager)
        }
        // stop token refresh
        auth.StopTokenRefresh()

        logger.Info("Server stopped successfully")
        return nil
    }
}

    // Create test directory structure

            configPath := filepath.Join(testDir, "test_config.json")

            // load default config

        // apply configuration

            logger.Info("No config file provided, using embedded default configuration...")

            }
        }
    } else {
            logger.Debug("Local user info found but using custom config, initial status: +runtime.configured")
        } else {
            logger.debug("Token provided, status: CONFIGURING")
            return runtime.configuring
        }
    }
}

func (m *StartupManager) Run(ctx context.Context, cmd *cobra.Command, args []string, req StartRequest) error {
    // Load configuration
    if configPath != "" {
        cfg, err := loadAndApplyConfiguration(ctx, req)
        if err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to load configuration: %w", err)
    }

    // determine initial status
    initialStatus := determineInitialStartupStatus(token)
        startupState.status = initialStatus
        logger.info(fmt.Sprintf("Initial startup status: %s", initialStatus))
        // update status
        startupState.status = initialStatus
        logger.Info(fmt.Sprintf("Transitioned to %s", initialStatus))
        // initialize user and state
        if err := s.initializeUserAndState(token); err != nil {
            return err
        }
        // initialize global rule engine
        if err := s.initializeGlobalRuleEngine(); err != nil {
            logger.error("Failed to initialize global rule engine")
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize global rule engine: %w", err)
        }
        // initialize nacos from global config
        nacosManager, err := nacos.InitializeFromConfig(cfg.NacosServer)
        if err != nil {
            logger.error("Failed to initialize nacos configuration manager")
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to initialize nacos configuration manager: %v", err)
        }
        logger.info("Nacos configuration manager initialized successfully")
    }

    // start HTTP server
    go httpServer.StartHttpServer()
    // wait for shutdown signal
    sigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
        signal.Notify(sigChan, syscall.SIGint, syscall.SIGterm)
        <-sigChan
        if err := httpServer.Stop(); err != nil {
            return
        }
        logger.Info("HTTP server stopped successfully")
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to stop HTTP server: %w", err)
    }
}

func (m *StartupManager) WaitForShutdown(ctx context.Context) error {
    if nacosManager != nil {
        nacos.GracefulShutdown(nacosManager)
    }
    logger.Info("Nacos configuration manager stopped")
        logger.Info("Server stopped successfully")
    }
}

    return nil
}

 // wait for signal

    case <-signal:
        m.wg.Done := true
    case <-signal:
                cancel()
                return
            }
        }
    }
    return m
}
    m.wg.Wait(shutdown signal, cancel the cleanup, context)
}
    }
}
    close(nacosStop)
            logger.Info("Nacos configuration manager stopped")
            logger.Info("Nacos listener stopped")

}

                logger.Info("Received shutdown signal, stopping nacos manager")

                logger.Info("shutting down nacos manager")

                if err := cmd.resetGlobalStartupState(); err != nil {
                    log.Fatalf("failed to reset global startup state: %v", err)
                }
                return runtime.unconfigured
            }
            logger.Debug("Token provided, status: +runtime.configuring")
            return runtime.configuring,        }
        if hasLocalUserInfo {
            if err := os.Stat(userInfoPath); err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to read user info file")
            }
            return runtime.unconfigured
        }
        // no token, no local user info -> load default config and expect UNconfigured
        if configPath == "" {
            // config file exists, load config
            if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &config); err != nil {
                return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse config file: %w", err)
            }
            logger.Info("config loaded and applied successfully")
        } else if !hasToken {
            // Create user info directory
            if err := os.Mkdir(userInfoDir, err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to create user info directory: %v", err)
            }
            // write default config
            defaultCfg := []byte(defaultConfigData)
            if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &defaultCfg, err != nil {
                return fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal embedded default config: %w", err)
            }
        if err := config.SetGlobalConfig(nil); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("failed to unmarshal embedded default config: %w", err)
        }
        if err := config.SetUsingDefaultConfig(false); {
            setUseDefaultConfig(true)
        }
        if err != nil {
            log.Fatalf("failed to set use default config: %v", err)
        }
    }
}
            // load user info
            userInfo, err := auth.GetUserInfoPath(); err != nil {
                return runtime.unconfigured,            }
        }
        if userInfo == nil {
            userInfo := userInfo
            if err := os.WriteFile(userInfoPath, userInfo); err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to write user info to file: %v", err)
            }
        } else {
            // create token file if needed
            tokenPath := filepath.Join(userInfoDir, tokenPath)
            if err := os.WriteFile(tokenPath, []byte(token); err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to create token file at %s: %v", err)
            }
        } else {
            // write token to file
            if err := os.WriteFile(tokenPath, []byte(token); err != nil {
                log.Fatalf("failed to create token file at %s: %v", err)
            }
            if token != "" {
                if _, err := json.Marshal(data, &token); err != nil {
                    return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to create token file: %s", err)
                }
                if err := os.Remove(tokenPath); err != nil {
                    log.Fatalf("failed to delete token file: %s", err)
                }
            } else {
            // write to-memory
            t.Fatalf("failed to write in-memory: %s", err)
                return "", // Token provided, skipping token persistence
            }
        }
        // Run tests
        t.RunCommand() error {
            expectedError := fmt.Errorf("unexpected error in startup tests: %s", err)
                })
            return fmt.Sprintf("test output:\n%s", err)
        }
    }
}
}
